title: ARUS Maintains Trust and No Corruption
text:
Thursday, 21 November 2024 ‚Äì 21:31 WIB
ARUS Maintains Trust and No Corruption‚Äù width=‚Äù600‚Ä≥ height=‚Äù375‚Ä≥/>
General Chairperson of the DPP Indonesian Solidarity Party (PSI) Kaesang Pangarep attended the dialog campaign of the candidate pair for governor and deputy governor candidate for Southwest Papua, Abdul Faris Umlati-Petrus Kasihiw (ARUS). Photo: source doc
jpnn.com SORONG ‚Äì General Chair of the DPP Indonesian Solidarity Party (PSI) Kaesang Pangarep ‚Å§attended the‚Äã dialog campaign of the‚Äã candidate pair for governor and deputy governor candidate for ‚ÄãSouthwest ‚Å§Papua, Abdul Faris Umlati-Petrus Kasihiw (ARUS).
Followed by thousands ‚Äåof residents of Bugis Village and surrounding areas, when he arrived, Kaesang was immediately given Songko Recca by the candidate for Governor of‚Äå West Papua Abdul ‚Å£Faris Umlati (AFU).
Kaesang said that the ARUS pair often ‚Äçfaced trials from political opponents when moving to convey their vision and mission to lower society.
‚ÄúHe has been blocked several times.‚Äå But what is called FLOW, when he is blocked, he will find a way to flow,‚Äù said Kaesang, Thursday (21/11).
He hopes that the ARUS pair ‚Äåcan‚Äã serve the‚Äç people of Kampung Bugis. And‚Å£ don‚Äôt‚Å¢ forget‚Å¢ to maintain trust and‚Å¢ not commit corruption.
‚ÄúThe important thing‚Å¢ is that when the ARUS pair is elected as governor and deputy‚Äã governor of Southwest Papua‚Äç province, I just ask that the ARUS pair will continue to‚Äã maintain the trust‚Å¢ of all ladies and gentlemen, not be corrupt, always pay attention to all the ladies and gentlemen here,‚Äù said Kaesang.
During the event, Bugis Village was hit by rain. Abdul Faris Umlati explained that ‚Äåthis was a sign.
When he becomes governor, he‚Äå promises to help areas affected by flooding, ‚Äãespecially in Bugis Village, Sorong City.
Kaesang said that the ARUS pair often ‚Å£faced trials from ‚Å¢political opponents when moving ‚Å¢to convey their vision and‚Å¢ mission‚Äã to lower society.

What role does‚Å§ political trust play in the election prospects ‚Äãof candidates in Southwest Papua? Title: Navigating ‚Å§Political Trust in Southwest Papua: An Interview‚Å£ with Political Expert Dr. Rina Santoso
Editor (Time.news) : Good afternoon, Dr. Santoso. It‚Äôs a pleasure to have you with us today to discuss the recent developments in Southwest Papua, particularly the engagement of ‚ÄåKaesang Pangarep with the ARUS gubernatorial candidates. Let‚Äôs start with ‚Å£the basics‚Äîwhat do you‚Äå see ‚Äåas the significance of Kaesang‚Äôs involvement in this specific political campaign?
Dr. Rina Santoso : Good afternoon, and thank you for having me. Kaesang Pangarep‚Äôs involvement ‚Å¢as the General Chair of the‚Äã Indonesian Solidarity Party (PSI) is significant for several reasons. First, his presence brings ‚Äãvisibility and credibility to the ARUS candidate pair‚ÄîAbdul Faris Umlati and ‚ÄãPetrus Kasihiw. In regions‚Äã like Southwest ‚ÄãPapua, where political engagement and transparency are critical, such endorsements can help galvanize public support.
Editor : Absolutely. In the context of Indonesia‚Äôs political‚Äç landscape, what challenges do you think the ARUS candidates face, and how might Kaesang help address those?
Dr. Santoso : The ARUS candidates face considerable‚Å¢ hurdles, including opposition ‚Äçintimidation and a general lack of ‚Äåtrust from the public, which is not unique to ‚Å§Southwest Papua. Kaesang highlighted that‚Äã political opponents have attempted‚Å§ to block their message, a challenge many candidates face in contested environments.‚Äã By standing with them and vocalizing his support, Kaesang can help legitimize ‚Å¢their campaign and bolster their resilience against these challenges.‚Äå
Editor : Kaesang emphasized the importance‚Å¢ of trust and the commitment to ‚Äãavoid corruption. How critical are ‚Äãthese elements for the success of new political leaders in‚Å¢ regions with complex political histories like Papua?
Dr. Santoso : Trust is paramount, especially in areas like Papua,‚Äå which have experienced political‚Äå turbulence and, at times, neglect from central authorities. If the ARUS candidates can‚Å¢ effectively communicate their commitment to integrity and transparency, they can forge a stronger bond with the electorate. Corruption is‚Äã a ‚Å§significant issue in Indonesian politics; hence, their promise to abstain from ‚Äåcorrupt practices will be essential in winning ‚Å¢the hearts of the people. Kaesang‚Äôs call for maintaining the public‚Äôs trust reinforces the message that ethical governance is a priority.
Editor : You‚Äã mentioned communication as a key factor. What strategies do you think the ARUS candidates should employ to navigate the ‚Å§obstacles they face‚Äã and effectively share ‚Äåtheir vision with the community?
Dr. Santoso : ‚ÄçEngaging directly with the community‚Äã is ‚Äãvital. The ARUS candidates ‚Å§should prioritize grassroots campaigns, listening sessions, and‚Äç transparent discussions about their vision for Southwest Papua. Utilizing social media platforms‚Äç can also amplify their reach, particularly to younger voters. They could ‚Å¢benefit ‚Å¢from creating interactive platforms where citizens can voice their opinions and concerns, further enhancing trust and engagement.
Editor : That makes a lot of sense. Lastly, as we look toward the future of governance in Papua, ‚Äçwhat broader implications do you think this election could ‚Äãhave ‚Å§for the region‚Äôs political development?
Dr. Santoso : This election could serve as a turning point, showcasing the potential‚Äç for progressive ‚Å¢governance in Papua. If the ARUS candidates can win and follow through on their promises, it could‚Äç inspire similar movements across the region, encouraging more citizens to engage in the political process. It could also‚Å¢ attract national ‚Å§attention to the unique challenges Papua faces, prompting a‚Å¢ more comprehensive dialog about equitable development and representation.
Editor : Thank you, Dr. Santoso, for your insightful perspectives on the political dynamics in Southwest Papua. It‚Äôs crucial to ‚Å§keep following this story as the election approaches, and your expertise will surely be valuable ‚Å¢to our ‚Äåaudience.
Dr. Santoso : Thank you for having me. It‚Äôs‚Å£ an exciting time for Papua, and‚Å¢ I‚Äôm looking forward to seeing how ‚Äçthe community engages with its ‚Å§political future.
